3A_Bibliographic.citation: Wade, N. E., Ortigara, J. M., Sullivan, R. M., Tomko, R. L., Breslin, F. J., Baker, F. C., Fuemmeler, B. F., Delrahim Howlett, K., Lisdahl, K. M., Marshall, A. T., Mason, M. J., Neale, M. C., Squeglia, L. M., Wolff-Hughes, D. L., Tapert, S. F., Bagot, K. S., & ABCD Novel Technologies Workgroup (2021). Passive sensing of preteens' smartphone use: An Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development (ABCD) cohort substudy. JMIR Mental Health, 8(10), e29426.

3B_Sample_Data.verbatim_sample_description: A total of 71 participant-parent dyads assented and consented to participate, respectively; 4 participants did not complete the full substudy protocol, and therefore, their data were not included in the present analyses. Thus, a total of 67 participants were enrolled in the study.
3B_Sample_Data.notes: This is a pilot substudy with a very small N=67

3C_Exposure.exposure_variable: Passive smartphone screen use (objective app-based measurement via EARS app) and self-reported/parent-reported smartphone use
3C_Exposure.exposure_instrument: Effortless Assessment of Risk States (EARS) app; self-report and parent-report screen time questionnaires
3C_Exposure.modalities: smartphone total use, streaming, YouTube, communication, gaming, social media, SMS, reading, music, news
3C_Exposure.operationalization: Continuous (minutes per day, objectively sensed via EARS app)
3C_Exposure.reporter: Device-based (EARS app objective measurement); also self-report and parent-report
3C_Exposure.verbatim_exposure_description: The EARS app ran continuously in the background of the participant's phone, scraping the operating system every few minutes to collect information on (1) screen on and off and (2) which app was in the foreground... Passive sensing data collection spanned an average of 33.91 (SD 22.20) days to collect the requisite 4 weeks of data.
3C_Exposure.notes: This is a measurement validation study, not a health outcome study

3D_Outcome.primary_outcome: Correlation between objective (EARS app) and self-reported smartphone screen use; feasibility and acceptability of passive sensing
3D_Outcome.outcome_instrument: EARS app (passive sensing); self-report and parent-report screen time questionnaires; acceptability questionnaires
3D_Outcome.verbatim_outcome_description: Pearson correlations were run to assess relationships between passive, objective sensing daily smartphone use and postsensing self- and parent-report measurements. Passive, objectively sensed, and self-reported daily smartphone screen use were significantly correlated (P<.001; r=0.49; 95% CI 0.28-0.66).
3D_Outcome.notes: This is primarily a feasibility/measurement study, not a screen time-to-health outcome study

3E_Design_Analysis.model_type_detail: Pearson correlations, paired t-tests, chi-square tests, ANOVA for demographic differences
3E_Design_Analysis.covariates_listed: age, sex, household income, race/ethnicity, geographic location
3E_Design_Analysis.verbatim_analysis_description: Summary descriptive statistics were calculated for total app use time, total app category time, and self- and parent-reported smartphone use. Analysis of variance evaluated potential differences in the most commonly used app categories by demographic factors. Pearson correlations were run to assess relationships between passive, objective sensing data and self- and parent-report measurements.

3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: Pearson correlation between objective and self-reported daily use
3F_Effect_Sizes.verbatim_quote: Passive, objectively sensed, and self-reported daily smartphone screen use were significantly correlated (P<.001; r=0.49; 95% CI 0.28-0.66)
Parent report of their child's smartphone screen use at both pre- (r=0.22) and postsensing (r=-0.06) did not significantly correlate with objective measurement or with children's self-report (P>.05).

3G_Reporting_Quality.notes: This is a measurement/feasibility study with N=67 pilot sample. Effect sizes not small - moderate correlation (r=0.49) between objective and self-report.

3H_Recommendations.notes: Paper recommends future research using passive sensing at larger scale but does not issue clinical/policy recommendations.

3I_Rhetorical_Moves.discussion_characterization: The discussion focuses on the methodological finding that children underreport smartphone use compared to objective measurement (r=0.49 moderate correlation) and that parent reports do not correlate significantly with objective data. It emphasizes the feasibility and acceptability of passive sensing in this age group and advocates for objective measurement to improve research accuracy. No clinical or policy recommendations regarding screen time effects on health.
3I_Rhetorical_Moves.ambiguity_notes: This is a measurement/feasibility study, not a screen time-to-health outcome study. It may not be a typical paper for this systematic review.
